data.title: Seven Seoul brands showcasing sustainability and innovation to Paris at Maison&Objet Paris 2025 by Seoul Design Foundation.
data.description: In Seoul, South Korea, the Seoul Design Foundation is set to showcase seven brands at Maison&Objet Paris 2025. These brands will highlight a fusion of Korean craftsmanship, technology, and urban creativity.

data.title: EU lawmakers show doubts about digital euro despite ECB's pitch for it as crisis backup; concerns over design and usage remain.
data.description: The digital euro has been proposed by Europe's central bank as a contingency plan for crises, however, certain EU lawmakers remain skeptical regarding its structure and utility.

data.title: Zendure enhances its Home Energy Management System with AI features and enters electric mobility market with e-cargo bike.
data.description: In a recent event in Berlin on September 5, 2025, Zendure, a prominent solar energy company, introduced an AI-enhanced Home Energy Management System (HEMS) with advanced features aimed at promoting smarter energy usage. The highlight of the presentation was the unveiling of a concept e-cargo bike as a component of the ZEN+ Home ecosystem. This move signifies Zendure's commitment to innovation in sustainable energy solutions.

data.title: CIM Group and Centennial Yards announce $5 billion downtown Atlanta development opening at The Mitchell Luxury Residential Tower.
data.description: A new luxury residential tower called The Mitchell has recently opened as part of a $5 billion development project in downtown Atlanta. The project was a collaboration between CIM Group and Centennial Yards Company.

data.title: Trump's steel tariffs impact gunmaker Smith & Wesson's Q1, with net loss of $3.4M & $85.1M sales. Company expects continued impact in Q2. CEO says firearms market normal.
data.description: Gunmaker Smith & Wesson Brands Inc. reported a net loss of $3.4 million, or $0.08 per share, in its first fiscal quarter, despite strong demand for new products. Steel tariffs imposed by the Trump administration resulted in a 120 basis point negative impact on gross margins, primarily from steel. This was on sales of $85.1 million. Chief Financial Officer Deana McPherson mentioned that the tariff impact is expected to persist, projecting the second-quarter gross margin to be in line with the first quarter's 25.9%. CEO Mark Smith noted that the firearms market remains "relatively normal" and cyclical, contrasting with previous surge years. The article provides further details on Benzinga.com.
